elements.sent-2411-001: We study rationality properties of real singular cubic threefolds.
elements.sent-2411-002: In this note we focus on rationality properties of singular cubic threefolds over the real numbers.
elements.sent-2411-003: We are guided by results in equivariant birational geometry in \cite{CTZ , \cite{CMTZ , which elucidated birationality constructions as well as obstructions to birationality, in the equivariant context.}}
elements.sent-2411-004: One of the main problems in birational geometry is the (stable) rationality problem; see, e.g., \cite{MT , \cite{CT-bir , \cite{Chel , \cite{Pro , \cite{AP , \cite{V , \cite{Schr for reports on milestones in this area.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Classically, one was interested in algebraically closed ground fields, of characteristic zero, mostly the complex numbers $\bC$.
elements.sent-2411-006: However, many (stable) rationality constructions rely crucially on results over nonclosed fields, e.g., function fields of smaller-dimensional varieties: in \cite{BCT this is used to produce nonrational but stably rational threefolds over $\bC$, via a degree 4 Del Pezzo fibration over $\bP^1$, and in \cite{AHTV rational cubic fourfolds, via a degree 6 Del Pezzo fibration over $\bP^2$.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: The survey \cite{CT-corps contains many results concerning rationality over arbitrary fields.}
elements.sent-2411-008: Of particular interest are varieties with simple presentations, such as hypersurfaces in (weighted) projective spaces, or linear sections of Grassmannians.
elements.sent-2411-009: Among the simplest such varieties are quadric and cubic hypersurfaces.
elements.sent-2411-010: Rationality of quadrics is completely settled: a necessary and sufficient condition is the existence of a rational point.
elements.sent-2411-011: For cubics, we do not currently have such criteria.
elements.sent-2411-012: Smooth complex cubic threefolds $X_{\bC}\subset \bP^4$ are irrational \cite{CG , while the singular ones are rational unless they are cones over smooth cubic curves.}
elements.sent-2411-013: In recent years, there have been several results concerning rationality of geometrically rational varieties over $\bR$.
elements.sent-2411-014: For example, it is now known that rationality of smooth intersections of two quadrics $X_{2,2}\subset \bP^n$, for $n=5$, is governed by the existence of rational points and lines \cite{HT-quad .}
elements.sent-2411-015: For $n=6$, a necessary and sufficient condition is that $X(\bR)$ is connected \cite{HKT-quad .}
elements.sent-2411-016: The papers \cite{BW , \cite{Ji-coni , \cite{CTA explore rationality of conic and quadric bundles over $\bR$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-017: Here, we focus on rationality of singular cubic threefolds \begin{equation}X=X_{\bR}\subset \bP^4\end{equation} over $\bR$.
elements.sent-2411-019: We recall standard rationality constructions, in presence of distinguished loci, over $\bR$:.
elements.sent-2411-020: Projection from a singular point gives a birational map to $\bP^3$,.
elements.sent-2411-021: Projection from a line and a disjoint plane gives a birational map $X\dashrightarrow \bP^2\times\bP^1$.
elements.sent-2411-022: In particular, if the cubic is not a cone and there exists a real singular point, then $X$ is rational over $\bR$.
elements.sent-2411-023: Hence, throughout the paper, we assume that $X$ is not a cone and that \begin{equation}X^{sing}(\bR)=\emptyset.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-024: This implies that either the singularities of $X_{\bC}$ are isolated and their number $s=s(X_{\bC )$ is even, i.e., $s=2,4,6,8,10$, or that the singular locus has positive dimension.}
elements.sent-2411-025: By \cite{Avilov-forms , a real form of a cubic with 10 isolated singularities is rational over $\bR$ -- geometrically, there is only one such cubic, the {\em Segre cubic.}}
elements.sent-2411-026: Let $\Gamma=\Gal(\bC/\bR)\simeq \bZ/2$ be the group generated by complex conjugation.
elements.sent-2411-027: In addition to an action of $\Gamma$ on special loci of $X$, such as the singular locus $X^{\mathrm{sing }$, we have an action on the geometric Picard group $\Pic(\tilde{X _{\bC )$ of the minimal resolution of singularities $\tilde{X}$ of $X$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-028: We investigate (stable) rationality over $\bR$, using the following tools:.

elements.sent-2411-248: The connected example also appeared in \cite[Section 11.3]{CTA .. \end{rema.}}
elements.sent-2411-249: A cubic $X_{\bC}$ singular along a rational normal quartic curve $C$ is unique: it is the secant variety of $C$, also known as the {\em chordal cubic , see, e.g., \cite{Allcub or \cite{yokocub .}}}
elements.sent-2411-250: Over $\bR$, and under the assumption $C(\bR)=\emptyset$, there is a unique such cubic $X$, given by \begin{equation}\label{eqn:chordal} \{x_1^2x_2 + x_1x_2^2 + x_2x_3^2 + x_1x_4^2 - 2x_3x_4x_5 - (x_1+x_2)x_5^2=0\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-252: The linear system $|\cO_X(2)-C|$ has dimension 5 and gives a contraction \begin{equation}\varphi:X\dashrightarrow S,\end{equation} where $S=\bP^2\hookrightarrow \bP^5$ is the Veronese surface.
elements.sent-2411-253: Projection from a general plane $\Pi$ in $\bP^4$ gives a rational map \begin{equation}\pi: X\dashrightarrow \bP^1.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-254: The product \begin{equation}\pi\times\varphi: X\dashrightarrow\bP^1\times\bP^2\end{equation} is a birational map.
